title: pulsatilla alpina subsp. alba
intro: Pulsatilla alpina subsp. alba, also known as the white pasque flower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Pulsatilla alpina subsp. alba include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which are derived from its roots and flowers. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat menstrual disorders, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its use as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Pulsatilla alpina subsp. alba is typically prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and balance.
